15-MINUTE CHART
The market then released to the downside. Around 9:00 AM, then specifically at 9:30 AM, the equity markets open. On the 15-minute time frame, at 9:30, the market runs a short-term high.

15-MINUTE TIME FRAME ANALYSIS
This is the old high or relative equal highs that were traded above on Monday. The price hugged the current New Week Opening Gap (NWOG). Here's the New Week Opening Gap High and New Week Opening Gap Low.

15-MINUTE CHART
On the 15-minute chart, we see the Fair Value Gap it worked up into. I wasn't interested in the back-and forth chopping action. When the 3915 and a quarter level broke, there was a gap that, on lower timeframes, was in my opinion, the better trade.

S&P 15-MINUTE
On Sunday and Monday of last week, we rallied up, straight back down, then an aggressive rally higher, and traded up into that higher inefficiency on the weekly chart (weekly volume imbalance). Then we worked lower, traded back up into a Balanced Price Range.

MARKET STRUCTURE AND TIMEFRAME ADJUSTMENT
A foundational market structure has been established: a low, high, and then a lower low within the range of a specific candle. This structure allows for a tactical adjustment before price potentially runs away.
